title: John Ringo

infobox.value: (1963-03-22) March 22, 1963 (age 63)  [Miami-Dade County, Florida](/wiki/Miami-Dade_County,_Florida "Miami-Dade County, Florida"), U.S.

Science fiction, [military science fiction](/wiki/Military_science_fiction "Military science fiction"), [military fiction](/wiki/War_novel "War novel"), [political thrillers](/wiki/Political_thriller "Political thriller"), [post apocalyptic](/wiki/Post_apocalyptic "Post apocalyptic"), [supernatural](/wiki/Supernatural "Supernatural")

(born March 22, 1963) is an American science fiction and [military fiction](/wiki/War_novel "War novel") author. He has had several *[New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* [best sellers](/wiki/The_New_York_Times_Best_Seller_list "The New York Times Best Seller list").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) His books range from straightforward science fiction to a mix of military and political thrillers. He has over seven million copies of his books in print, and his works have been translated into seven different languages.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

["New York Times Best Seller List, April 17, 2005"](https://www.nytimes.com/2005/04/17/books/bestseller/0417besthardfiction.html). *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")*.
["Hardcover Fiction Books – Best Sellers – Books – Jan. 23, 2011 – The New York Times"](https://www.nytimes.com/books/best-sellers/2011/01/23/hardcover-fiction/). *The New York Times*.
["Hardcover Fiction Books – Best Sellers – Books – May 29, 2011 – The New York Times"](https://www.nytimes.com/books/best-sellers/2011/05/29/hardcover-fiction/). *The New York Times*.

["Baen Authors: John Ringo"](https://web.archive.org/web/20150930214901/http://www.baen.com/author_catalog.asp?author=JRingo). Archived from [the original](http://www.baen.com/author_catalog.asp?author=jringo) on September 30, 2015.

["M Is for Mine"](http://www.johnringo.com/Opeds/Misformine/tabid/1629/language/en-US/Default.aspx); [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20090123220200/http://www.johnringo.com/Opeds/Misformine/tabid/1629/language/en-US/Default.aspx) January 23, 2009, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), *[New York Post](/wiki/New_York_Post "New York Post")*, reprinted on www.johnringo.com.

["First... We Remove All the Lawyers"](http://www.johnringo.com/Opeds/Oped%20docs/Firstwe.asp); [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20080905133038/http://www.johnringo.com/Opeds/Oped%20docs/Firstwe.asp) September 5, 2008, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), *[New York Post](/wiki/New_York_Post "New York Post")*, reprinted on www.johnringo.com.

["Home of the Brave"](http://www.johnringo.com/Opeds/Oped%20docs/Homeofthebrave.asp); [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20080905132019/http://www.johnringo.com/Opeds/Oped%20docs/Homeofthebrave.asp) September 5, 2008, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), *[New York Post](/wiki/New_York_Post "New York Post")*, reprinted on www.johnringo.com.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American science fiction and military fiction writer (born 1963)

For other people named Johnny Ringo, see [Johnny Ringo (disambiguation)](/wiki/Johnny_Ringo_(disambiguation) "Johnny Ringo (disambiguation)").

| John Ringo | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | (1963-03-22) March 22, 1963 (age 63)  [Miami-Dade County, Florida](/wiki/Miami-Dade_County,_Florida "Miami-Dade County, Florida"), U.S. |
| Occupation | Novelist, author |
| Genre | Science fiction, [military science fiction](/wiki/Military_science_fiction "Military science fiction"), [military fiction](/wiki/War_novel "War novel"), [political thrillers](/wiki/Political_thriller "Political thriller"), [post apocalyptic](/wiki/Post_apocalyptic "Post apocalyptic"), [supernatural](/wiki/Supernatural "Supernatural") |
| Spouse | Miriam Ringo |
| Children | 2 |
| Website | |
| [johnringoauthor.substack.com](https://johnringoauthor.substack.com/) | |

**John Ringo** (born March 22, 1963) is an American science fiction and [military fiction](/wiki/War_novel "War novel") author. He has had several *[New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* [best sellers](/wiki/The_New_York_Times_Best_Seller_list "The New York Times Best Seller list").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) His books range from straightforward science fiction to a mix of military and political thrillers. He has over seven million copies of his books in print, and his works have been translated into seven different languages.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Early life and military career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and military career")]

Ringo's father "was a [civil engineer](/wiki/Civil_engineer "Civil engineer") with an international firm";[[5]](#cite_note-SFRevu-5) before Ringo graduated in 1981 from [Winter Park High School](/wiki/Winter_Park_High_School "Winter Park High School") in [Winter Park, Florida](/wiki/Winter_Park,_Florida "Winter Park, Florida"), he had spent time in 23 foreign countries, attending classes at fourteen schools. Among the countries he spent the most time in were Greece, Iran and Switzerland before he settled with his parents and six siblings in [Alabama](/wiki/Alabama "Alabama"). This amount of travel brought what he refers to as a "wonderful appreciation of the oneness of humanity and a permanent aversion to foreign food."[[6]](#cite_note-About-6)

After graduation, Ringo joined the [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") and rose to the rank of [Specialist](/wiki/Specialist_(rank)#Specialist_(1955–present) "Specialist (rank)") in the [82nd Airborne Division](/wiki/82nd_Airborne_Division "82nd Airborne Division"). During his four years of active duty, he was assigned to the 1st Battalion, [508th Parachute Infantry Regiment](/wiki/508th_Infantry_Regiment_(United_States) "508th Infantry Regiment (United States)"), reflagged into 3rd Battalion, [505th Parachute Infantry Regiment](/wiki/505th_Infantry_Regiment_(United_States) "505th Infantry Regiment (United States)") when the 82nd reorganized its 3rd Brigade, plus two years of reserve duty with the Florida [National Guard](/wiki/United_States_National_Guard "United States National Guard"). Among his awards are the [Combat Infantryman Badge](/wiki/Combat_Infantryman_Badge "Combat Infantryman Badge"), [Parachutist Badge](/wiki/Parachutist_Badge_(United_States) "Parachutist Badge (United States)"), [Army Commendation Medal](/wiki/Commendation_Medal#Army "Commendation Medal"), [Good Conduct Medal](/wiki/Good_Conduct_Medal_(United_States) "Good Conduct Medal (United States)"), [Armed Forces Expeditionary Medal](/wiki/Armed_Forces_Expeditionary_Medal "Armed Forces Expeditionary Medal") for his participation in the 1983 [United States invasion of Grenada](/wiki/United_States_invasion_of_Grenada "United States invasion of Grenada"), and the [National Defense Service Medal](/wiki/National_Defense_Service_Medal "National Defense Service Medal").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

After discharge, Ringo earned an [associate degree](/wiki/Associate_degree "Associate degree") in [marine biology](/wiki/Marine_biology "Marine biology"). However, he quickly discovered that marine biology would only "pay for beans"[[6]](#cite_note-About-6) and became a [database](/wiki/Database "Database") manager to support his wife and two daughters.

## Writing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Writing career")]

In 1999, he had the idea for a science fiction story that involved an alien invasion and a military response that became the novel *[A Hymn Before Battle](/wiki/A_Hymn_Before_Battle "A Hymn Before Battle")*, the title referring to [Rudyard Kipling](/wiki/Rudyard_Kipling "Rudyard Kipling")'s poem "[Hymn Before Action](/wiki/Hymn_Before_Action "Hymn Before Action")", quoted extensively throughout the book.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The success of the book and its sequels allowed Ringo to quit his job and become a full-time writer. As of 2015, John Ringo had written 46 novels, some with co-authors [David Weber](/wiki/David_Weber "David Weber"), [Michael Z. Williamson](/wiki/Michael_Z._Williamson "Michael Z. Williamson"), Julie Cochrane, [Linda Evans](/wiki/Linda_Evans_(author) "Linda Evans (author)"), [Travis S. Taylor](/wiki/Travis_S._Taylor "Travis S. Taylor"), and [Tom Kratman](/wiki/Tom_Kratman "Tom Kratman").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

He has also written a number of op-ed pieces for the *[New York Post](/wiki/New_York_Post "New York Post")*,[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) and been a guest commentator for [Fox News](/wiki/Fox_News "Fox News") and [National Geographic](/wiki/National_Geographic_(U.S._TV_channel) "National Geographic (U.S. TV channel)").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In 2012, he was presented with the Phoenix Award at [DeepSouthCon](/wiki/DeepSouthCon "DeepSouthCon") 50 in Huntsville, Alabama, in recognition of his contributions to science fiction literature.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Ringo coined the anti–[political correctness](/wiki/Political_correctness "Political correctness") slogan "[get woke, go broke](/wiki/Get_woke,_go_broke "Get woke, go broke")",[[14]](#cite_note-14) citing political tensions in a fan convention following the [2000 United States presidential election](/wiki/2000_United_States_presidential_election "2000 United States presidential election") and declining interest in organizations that embrace progressive cultural shifts. The term suggests that organizations that embrace [social justice](/wiki/Social_justice "Social justice") initiatives ("getting [woke](/wiki/Woke "Woke")") will drive away significant, mostly conservative or apolitical fans and their interests, and thus lose money ("going broke").

In March of 2023 John Ringo started his own substack "Ringo's Tavern"[[15]](#cite_note-15) for his writing. Saying he wanted to continue doing traditional publishing but also nature of publishing was changing this was his way of changing with it.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Works")]

Main article: [John Ringo bibliography](/wiki/John_Ringo_bibliography "John Ringo bibliography")

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [The Crüxshadows](/wiki/The_Cr%C3%BCxshadows "The Crüxshadows"), mentioned in the *Paladin of Shadows* series; the protagonist makes numerous mentions of the song "Winterborn" in particular.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The main characters in *Claws That Catch* also play "Return" in order to defeat the aliens.[[18]](#cite_note-18) The book *Eye of the Storm* quotes the song of the same name a few times. In the novel *Von Neumann's War*, the song "Citadel" is the anthem of the soldiers and it is played during the final showdown. The *Black Tide Rising* series also quotes the Crüxshadows extensively.
* *[Schlock Mercenary](/wiki/Schlock_Mercenary "Schlock Mercenary")*, a [webcomic](/wiki/Webcomic "Webcomic") set far into the future. The *Troy Rising* series is inspired by the universe of *[Schlock Mercenary](/wiki/Schlock_Mercenary "Schlock Mercenary")* at the point of first contact. Also, a number of characters in the *Black Tide Rising* series state that they are fans of the *Schlock* webcomic, and often offer variations on the comic's "Rule 37" ("There is no 'overkill.' There is only 'open fire' and 'I need to reload!'").
* *[Sluggy Freelance](/wiki/Sluggy_Freelance "Sluggy Freelance")*, a [webcomic](/wiki/Webcomic "Webcomic") featured in the Posleen Series books; a SheVa tank is named after the character Bun-bun. A character styled after Bun-bun is featured in *The Council Wars* series.[[19]](#cite_note-19)
